health_zone_table.basis: SitRep50 Table 2 (03 July 2026) visually reviewed against the INSP PDF (post 25088; Draft_SitRep_MVE_RDC_N°050_04_07_2026 sha256 37880c6d4af5da91940d7a6afbf049cb6ca84c5d972ce1d58d479775e6160864, 13 pages). Header/slug carries MVB_02/07/2026 but the PDF body and Tableau report date is 03 July 2026 (publication 04 July 2026); the body/table date is authoritative.

reconciliation.note: Rows promoted here reconcile to official province/national totals: 1511 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation = 1528; all 492 confirmed deaths are named-zone attributed. The residual row is retained as unventilated context and excluded from mappable named-zone attribution.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate from SitRep50 section 4.3: Ituri 54 collected/analyzed / 21 positive (38.8%); Nord-Kivu 93 collected/analyzed / 5 positive (5.4%); Sud-Kivu 5 collected and still under analysis (excluded from positivity). Positivity uses 26 positives over 147 analyzed samples.

care_continuity: SitRep50 re-reports the aggregate isolation census at 628 patients (89.4% occupancy) in the banner, unchanged from SitRep49's end-of-day census, but published NO fresh patient-movement table (Tableau 6) this cycle. The confirmed/suspected split (215 confirmed / 413 suspected) is therefore carried from SitRep49's last published split, which still reconciles to the 628 census (215 + 413 = 628); it is a carried/reconciled estimate, not a freshly reported table. Nord-Kivu isolation runs at 136% occupancy (challenge table cites 138.8% saturation).

Continuity of care reports 109 confirmed people-deprived-of-liberty (PPL) cumulative in Ituri, of whom 34 died (31.2%) and 46 recovered. 774 hot meals were served (150 to confirmed cases, 127 to suspected cases, 446 to PPLs, 52 to caregivers).
highlights: 26 new confirmed cases including 9 community deaths were reported for 2026-07-03, in Ituri (21 including 6 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (5 including 3 deaths). In total 19 confirmed deaths were recorded over the day: the 9 community deaths plus 10 deaths among previously confirmed CTE/CT patients (8 Ituri, 2 Nord-Kivu Katwa), bringing confirmed deaths to 492.
The 26 new cases came from Rwampara (11), Bunia (5), Mongbwalu (3) and Mangala (2) in Ituri and from Butembo (2), Musienene (1), Masereka (1) and Beni (1) in Nord-Kivu.
No new affected health zone was reported on 2026-07-03; affected zones remain 36/104 and Ituri stays at 24/36 zones touched. Sud-Kivu has recorded no confirmed case since 2026-05-26.

Nord-Kivu cumulative counts were re-actualized this cycle: alongside 5 new confirmations the province table shows a within-province reclassification (Goma 2->1, Kyondo 3->4), a net +5 consistent with DHIS2 cleaning/harmonization.

10 patients recovered (9 Ituri, 1 Nord-Kivu Katwa), bringing DRC cumulative recoveries to 239. Mongbwalu carries a 50.4% case-fatality (139 deaths for 276 cases); Nord-Kivu remains the highest-lethality province at 58.0%, led by Katwa (72.5%), Oicha (66.7%) and Beni (58.6%).
laboratory: SitRep50 reports Ituri collected and analyzed 54 samples with 21 positive (38.8%).

Nord-Kivu collected and analyzed 93 samples with 5 positive (5.4%). Sud-Kivu collected 5 samples still under analysis. New diagnostic labs are being installed at Komanda, Nizi, Mambasa and Nia-Nia (Ituri).
publication_context: INSP SitRep 50 reports through 2026-07-03 and was published on the INSP WordPress site (post 25088; embedded PDF media 25089; WP publish 2026-07-04T19:32:31Z). The PDF title and slug carry SitRep N°050/MVB_02/07/2026, but the PDF body and Tableau report date is 03 July 2026 (publication 04 July 2026); the body/table date is authoritative.
SitRep50 supersedes SitRep49 directly (a single report-day advance); the cumulative rose by 26 confirmed and 19 deaths over 2026-07-03.
The outbreak is reported across 3 provinces and 36 of 104 health zones; no newly affected zone was reported this SitRep.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 1387 of 1528 DRC confirmed cases (90.7%) and 411 deaths (CFR 29.6%).
Nord-Kivu holds the highest provincial confirmed-case fatality ratio at 58.0%, led by Katwa (72.5%), Oicha (66.7%) and Beni (58.6%); 100% lethality observed in low-count Vuhovi.
redactions: Page contact details and operational names, if present outside table facts, are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 1131 alerts of the day were reported (60 J-1 + 1071 new) and 902 investigated (Tableau 3), a 79.7% investigation rate; Ituri 34.2% (119/348), Nord-Kivu 100.0% (768/768), Sud-Kivu 100.0% (15/15).
185 suspected cases of the day were identified, including 67 deceased validated alerts.

9971 contacts were under follow-up and 8126 were seen, producing 81.5% follow-up coverage; Ituri 82.3%, Nord-Kivu 78.9%. The rate is computed only for reporting ZS (completeness 90%); Bunia, Aungba and Nia-Nia (Ituri) had no contact data at consolidation.

329 new contacts were listed (132 across 4 Ituri health zones and 197 across 3 Nord-Kivu health zones); 501 contacts completed 21-day follow-up (384 in Ituri, 117 in Nord-Kivu); 44 contacts became suspects (all in Ituri).

A dead body arriving from Uganda toward the DRC (Beni) was intercepted at the Kasindi PoE; 3 dead bodies were intercepted at PoE/PoC overall. A security incident (vandalism) was reported at PoC Irumu Centre.

Community-death analysis (SitRep50): the majority of recorded deaths are community deaths, concentrated in Mongbwalu, Bunia and Katwa, with late presentation to health structures raising the risk of secondary household transmission. These sub-cohort figures are qualitative context and are not used as denominators for the 492 confirmed-death total.

alerts_total.data_gap_note: Report (J-1) 60; new alerts 1071; total alerts of the day 1131. Tableau 3 investigated sum = 902 (79.7%). 329 new contacts listed; 501 completed 21-day follow-up; 44 contacts became suspects (all in Ituri).

challenges.finding: Community reluctance toward post-mortem sampling (EDS/swabs) and resistance delay confirmation, underestimate cases, and disrupt key activities.
challenges.response: Intensify RCCE, involve religious and community leaders, and reinforce team security.

challenges.finding: Insufficient CTE capacity with Nord-Kivu saturation cited at 138.8% in the challenge table; the banner reports national isolation occupancy 89.4% (628 patients), raising nosocomial-transmission risk.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction/expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up performance reached 81.5% (challenge table cites 81.3%) versus the 95% target, leaving transmission chains uninterrupted.
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Weak alert reporting in some affected health zones risks undetected, silent transmission.
challenges.response: Reinforce community-based surveillance and rapid alert investigation.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet ventilated by health zone, leaving gaps in transmission-chain reconstruction.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, prune duplicates, and reclassify/localize with active field verification.

challenges.finding: Insufficient supply of essential medicines (MEG) and medical inputs weakens holistic case management.
challenges.response: Advocacy and urgent procurement of essential medicines.

challenges.finding: Insufficient IPC inputs (PPE, chlorine) and limited training raise nosocomial-infection risk; the challenge table reports 96 health-worker infections and 19 deaths (CFR 19.7%).
challenges.response: Urgent procurement and reinforced IPC training.

challenges.finding: A backlog of un-analyzed samples in Nord-Kivu and late diagnosis persist amid a 54.2% provincial CFR (challenge table).
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

challenges.finding: Insufficient ambulances and isolation structures (a gap of about 20 centers) delay transfer and isolation and raise transmission risk.
challenges.response: Urgent deployment of logistics and additional infrastructure.

challenges.finding: Insufficient coordination and weak use of COUSP frameworks cause operational inefficiency and delayed decisions.
challenges.response: Reinforce coordination mechanisms at all levels and operationalize provincial COUSP.

challenges.finding: A financial-resource gap of about USD 20 million limits implementation across all response pillars.
challenges.response: Urgent resource mobilization and partner advocacy.

challenges.finding: Insecurity and limited access (CODECO, ADF) restrict operations and access to high-risk zones.
challenges.response: Strengthen security coordination and humanitarian access.

challenges.finding: Compromised continuity of essential (non-Ebola) health services raises indirect mortality.
challenges.response: Effectively implement free care and reinforce continuity of essential health services (CEHS).

challenges.finding: High population mobility raises the risk of rapid geographic expansion; this cycle a dead body arriving from Uganda toward the DRC was intercepted at the Kasindi PoE (Beni, Nord-Kivu), and the Wamba/Haut-Uele imported cases stay counted within Nia-Nia (Ituri).
challenges.response: Reinforce PoE/PoC surveillance and cross-border/inter-provincial coordination.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: 329 new contacts listed (132 in 4 Ituri HZs, 197 in 3 Nord-Kivu HZs); 501 completed 21-day follow-up (384 Ituri, 117 Nord-Kivu); 44 became suspects (all Ituri). Follow-up rate computed only for reporting ZS (completeness 90%); Bunia, Aungba and Nia-Nia (Ituri) had no contact data at consolidation. Sud-Kivu contacts remain exited. Challenge table cites follow-up at 81.3%.

patient_movement_total.data_gap_note: SitRep50 re-reports the aggregate isolation census 628 at 89.4% occupancy in the banner (unchanged from SitRep49's end-of-day 628/89.4%) but published NO fresh patient-movement (CTE/CT/CI) table this cycle. The confirmed/suspected split (215 confirmed / 413 suspected) is carried from SitRep49's last published Tableau 6 split, which still reconciles to the 628 census (215 + 413 = 628); the split is a carried/reconciled estimate, not a freshly reported table. Exit flows sourced from the holistic-care section: 10 recoveries (9 Ituri, 1 Nord-Kivu Katwa) and 10 deaths among previously confirmed CTE/CT patients (8 Ituri, 2 Nord-Kivu Katwa); other 24h movement flows were not published and are shown as 0.

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones; keep the Wamba/Haut-Uele imported cases counted within Nia-Nia

Relieve isolation load at 89.4% national occupancy and the Nord-Kivu 58.0% CFR; close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers and accelerate CTE construction (new CTE site identified in Bunia; Nizi and Nyankunde sites evaluated)
Improve contact follow-up (81.5% vs 95% target), reinforce intervention teams and transport in affected ZS, and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog

preserved_conflicts: SitRep50 headline is DRC-only (1528 confirmed / 492 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope public snapshot adds the latest reviewed Uganda component separately.

The 1,528 confirmed total is marked re-actualized and subject to change after DHIS2 cleaning/reclassification/harmonization; SitRep50 supersedes SitRep49 directly (a single report-day advance), so treat the +26 confirmed and +19 confirmed deaths vs SitRep49 as an official one-day delta for 2026-07-03.

Nord-Kivu cumulative counts were re-actualized: alongside 5 new confirmations the province table carries a within-province reclassification (Goma 2->1, Kyondo 3->4), a net +5 change consistent with data cleaning rather than 5 additional field notifications plus a lost case.
SitRep50 carries an explicit Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified; these counts are public but not mappable to named health zones.
No new affected health zone was reported this cycle; the affected-zone count remains 36 of 104 (Ituri 24/36). No new map geometry was required.

The isolation census (628 at 89.4% occupancy) is re-reported only in the SitRep50 banner and is unchanged from SitRep49's end-of-day census; SitRep50 published NO fresh patient-movement (CTE/CT/CI) table, so the confirmed/suspected split (215 confirmed / 413 suspected) is carried from SitRep49's last published Tableau 6 split and still reconciles to the 628 census (215 + 413 = 628). It is a carried/reconciled estimate, not a freshly reported table. Nord-Kivu isolation occupancy is 136% (138.8% saturation cited in the challenge table).
Daily suspected cases (185) and the 67 deceased validated alerts are current-cycle validated alert outputs, not cumulative suspected stock or cumulative confirmed deaths.

Uganda anchor remains 20 cumulative confirmed cases (2 deaths, 11 recoveries) per the Uganda MoH EVD daily dashboard (last reviewed 2026-06-21); WHO AFRO Uganda External SitRep 07 (data as of 2026-06-28) independently reports no new confirmed Uganda cases over the past week. Carried forward this cycle as INSP reports DRC only, so country-scope is 1548 confirmed / 494 deaths / 250 recovered.

Sud-Kivu has recorded no new confirmed case since 2026-05-26, suggesting no recent transmission; it remains at 3 confirmed / 1 death across Miti-Murhesa, and its contacts remain exited from follow-up.
Several low-denominator zones report 100% case-fatality this cycle (Drodro 3/3, Kambala 2/2, Vuhovi 1/1, Lolwa 1/1); these are unstable small-number ratios.

The challenge table cites 96 health-worker infections with 19 deaths (CFR 19.7%), a Nord-Kivu CTE saturation of 138.8%, a Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog amid a 54.2% provincial CFR, an isolation-structure gap of about 20 centers, and a financing gap of about USD 20 million.

A community-death sub-analysis in SitRep50 (majority of deaths occurring in the community, concentrated in Mongbwalu, Bunia and Katwa; late presentation to care) is internally inconsistent with the cumulative confirmed-death total and is treated as qualitative context only, never as a denominator.

External response milestones reported outside the INSP SitRep are tracked as separate context in the LOVS narrative/synthesis, not in the INSP denominators: the WHO Emergency Use Listing of the first BDBV molecular RT-PCR diagnostic (2026-07-02) supports the SitRep-noted decentralization of diagnostic capacity (new labs at Komanda, Nizi, Mambasa, Nia-Nia); and the response financing picture (INSP-cited ~USD 20 million pillar-implementation gap; separately, an Africa CDC response requirement of ~USD 1.4 billion with pledges only ~13% released, a White House/OMB supplemental request of ~USD 1.4 billion still before Congress, and OCHA/UNDP macro-humanitarian and socioeconomic context) is recorded as distinct facets and not collapsed into a single figure.

review_notes: SitRep #050 (report date 2026-07-03; INSP WordPress post 25088 published 2026-07-04T19:32:31Z; embedded PDF media 25089). PDF sha256 37880c6d4af5da91940d7a6afbf049cb6ca84c5d972ce1d58d479775e6160864, 13 pages. Title/slug N°050/MVB_02/07/2026; the PDF body/Tableau report date is 03 July 2026 (publication 04 July 2026), which is authoritative over the slug's 02/07 metadata.

SitRep50 supersedes SitRep49 directly (single report-day). DRC headline 1528 confirmed / 492 confirmed deaths (CFR 32.2%); +26 confirmed and +19 confirmed deaths vs SitRep49. On 03 July, 26 new confirmed (Ituri 21, Nord-Kivu 5) carried 9 community deaths; a further 10 deaths occurred among previously confirmed CTE/CT patients (8 Ituri, 2 Nord-Kivu Katwa), for +19 total confirmed deaths.

The 1,528 total is flagged re-actualized and subject to change after DHIS2 cleaning. Nord-Kivu carries a within-province reclassification (Goma 2->1, Kyondo 3->4) alongside 5 new confirmations, netting +5.
No new affected health zone this cycle. Affected-zone count remains 36 of 104; Ituri stays 24/36 touched. No new map geometry/centroid required.
Health-zone rows reconcile to 1528 confirmed / 492 deaths: 1511 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation, with all 492 confirmed deaths named-zone attributed.

Recovered advanced to 239 DRC cumulative (+10; 9 Ituri, 1 Nord-Kivu). Uganda anchor carried forward at 20 cumulative cases / 2 deaths / 11 recoveries (last reviewed 2026-06-21), so country-scope is 1548 confirmed / 494 deaths / 250 recovered.

Laboratory throughput promoted from section 4.3: Ituri 54 collected/analyzed, 21 positive (38.8%); Nord-Kivu 93 collected/analyzed, 5 positive (5.4%); Sud-Kivu 5 collected and still under analysis (147 analyzed / 26 positive overall, 17.7%).

SitRep50 published NO fresh patient-movement table; the banner re-reports the isolation census 628 at 89.4% (unchanged from SitRep49). The confirmed/suspected split (215/413) is carried from SitRep49 and reconciles to 628. Operational data captured: alerts 1131 of the day / 902 investigated (79.7%), 185 suspected cases of the day (67 deceased validated alerts), 9971 contacts under follow-up (81.5%, completeness 90%), 329 new contacts, 501 completed 21-day.

External context recorded separately (not in INSP denominators): WHO Emergency Use Listing of the first BDBV molecular diagnostic (2026-07-02); financing facets (INSP ~USD 20M pillar gap; Africa CDC ~USD 1.4B requirement / ~13% released; White House/OMB ~USD 1.4B request pending; OCHA/UNDP macro context) are kept as distinct facets.
